title: asperula prostrata
intro: Asperula prostrata, also known as creeping cinquefoil, is a low-growing perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Asia. This plant contains iridoid glycosides, which exhibit medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Asperula prostrata include its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea, and to support the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal usage.
